excluded_defs.reason: read: checked how Match handles ErrMethodMismatch and when it returns true vs false; no change needed here

exploration_log.aha_moment: The match-success branch always sets requestWithRouter (when !omitRouterFromContext) but the 405 branch doesn't. Adding requestWithRouter to the 405 branch mirrors the same guard already used in the success path.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix is a 3-line addition. The omitRouterFromContext guard must be respected to be consistent with the match-success path.

alternatives: Set router context before the Match() call
Set router context inside the 405 branch
key_decisions.decision: Set router context inside the 405 branch only
key_decisions.reasoning: Setting it before Match() would also set it for 404 cases which don't need it; the 405 branch is the specific case requiring the fix

solve_notes: In ServeHTTP, added requestWithRouter(req, r) inside the `if handler == nil && match.MatchErr == ErrMethodMismatch` branch (guarded by omitRouterFromContext). This mirrors the existing context-population logic in the match-success branch and ensures middleware and the 405 handler can call CurrentRouter(r).
task_text:
Fix Router.ServeHTTP not setting request context for method-not-allowed handlers

In `mux.go`, when `Match()` returns `false` with
`match.MatchErr == ErrMethodMismatch`, the 405 handler is invoked
without the router context set on the request. Middleware and the
`MethodNotAllowedHandler` cannot call `mux.CurrentRouter(r)` because
`requestWithRouter` is only called in the match-success branch. Fix
`ServeHTTP` to call `requestWithRouter` before dispatching the 405
handler so the router is available in the request context.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: tests that method-not-allowed handler is invoked when method doesn't match; exercises the ErrMethodMismatch code path in ServeHTTP
tests method-not-allowed handling with middleware to verify the middleware chain executes correctly in the 405 path

thrash_preventing_defs.name: requestWithRouter
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: signature and body confirms it takes router and sets routerKey in context; verifies it's the right function to call

thrash_preventing_defs.name: CurrentRouter
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: retrieves routerKey from context; confirms what callers of the 405 handler would use (validates the fix is complete)

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just ServeHTTP. The thrash_preventing set adds requestWithRouter (to confirm it sets the correct context key and has the right signature) and CurrentRouter (to verify the fix actually enables what the task describes). Without these, an agent might search for what function to call or whether there's a different way to set the router context.
